questions.question: Describe the structure and working of an artificial neuron with its mathematical formulation.
keyPoints: Components: inputs, weights, bias, activation, output

questions.question: Compare four common activation functions: step, sigmoid, tanh, and ReLU.
keyPoints: ReLU: fast, no vanishing gradient for positive, dead neuron risk

questions.question: Draw the analogy between biological and artificial neurons.
Explain the role of the bias term in an artificial neuron.
keyPoints: Enables non-zero output with zero inputs
Provides additional flexibility to the model
Analogous to intercept in linear equation
